# Generated Files Reference

This page documents every file that RiverGen writes, who owns it after generation, and which gates enforce its shape. Files fall into three categories:

- **Init files** — written once by `rivergen init`, never touched again by `rivergen gen`.
- **Domain files** — written per domain by `rivergen gen`, not overwritten on subsequent runs without `--force`.
- **Barrel files** — regenerated by `rivergen gen` on every run, always overwritten.

---

## Generated vs User-Owned Boundary

| File                              | Written by     | Developer fills                                                               | Gate(s)           |
| --------------------------------- | -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------- |
| `event-factory.service.ts`        | `init` (once)  | Nothing — do not modify                                                       | —                 |
| `event-bus.service.ts`            | `init` (once)  | Nothing — do not modify                                                       | —                 |
| `schemas/_base.ts`                | `init` (once)  | Nothing — do not modify                                                       | —                 |
| `entity-cache.ts`                 | `init` (once)  | Nothing — do not modify                                                       | Gate #4           |
| `state-cache.ts`                  | `init` (once)  | Nothing — do not modify                                                       | —                 |
| `WebSocketProvider.tsx`           | `init` (once)  | Nothing — do not modify                                                       | Gate #8           |
| `entity-projections/_types.ts`    | `init` (once)  | Nothing — do not modify                                                       | —                 |
| Vite proxy shims (`index.ts` × 3) | `init` (once)  | Nothing — do not modify                                                       | —                 |
| `AGENTS.md` / `CLAUDE.md`         | `init` (once)  | Nothing — do not modify                                                       | —                 |
| `schemas/_index.ts`               | `gen` (barrel) | Nothing — always overwritten                                                  | —                 |
| `domain-dispatchers/_index.ts`    | `gen` (barrel) | Nothing — always overwritten                                                  | —                 |
| `ws-bindings/_index.ts`           | `gen` (barrel) | Nothing — always overwritten                                                  | —                 |
| `query-keys/_index.ts`            | `gen` (barrel) | Nothing — always overwritten                                                  | —                 |
| `entity-projections/_index.ts`    | `gen` (barrel) | Nothing — always overwritten                                                  | —                 |
| `<domain>.router.ts`              | `gen` (domain) | Auth middleware, if needed                                                    | —                 |
| `<domain>.mutations.ts`           | `gen` (domain) | DB call, input validation, full payload                                       | Gate #1           |
| `<domain>.broadcast.ts`           | `gen` (domain) | Nothing when `privateRoomTemplate` is set; private room expression if omitted | Gate #5           |
| `<domain>.listener.ts`            | `gen` (domain) | Nothing — complete as generated                                               | Gate #2           |
| `schemas/<domain>.ts`             | `gen` (domain) | All payload fields beyond `entityId`                                          | Gate #7, Gate #12 |
| `<domain>-projections.ts`         | `gen` (domain) | Context object (projectId, workspaceId, etc.)                                 | Gate #4           |
| `use-<domain>.ts`                 | `gen` (domain) | API call URLs, entity types                                                   | Gate #9, Gate #10 |
| `domain-dispatchers/<domain>.ts`  | `gen` (domain) | Nothing — complete as generated                                               | Gate #3           |
| `ws-bindings/<domain>.ts`         | `gen` (domain) | Nothing — complete as generated                                               | —                 |
| `entity-projections/<domain>.ts`  | `gen` (domain) | Correct key factories matching onMutate list key shape                        | —                 |
| `query-keys/<domain>.ts`          | `gen` (domain) | Rarely — generated correctly in most cases                                    | —                 |
| `<domain>.witness.ts`             | `gen` (domain) | TaskPayload interface, requiredFields, testPayloads, lifecycle()              | Gate #12          |

---

## Init Files

Init files are written once by `rivergen init`. They form the static infrastructure that all domain code depends on. `rivergen gen` never touches them. Do not modify any of these files — every gate assumes they are intact and unaltered.

### `event-factory.service.ts`

**Path:** `apps/api/src/lib/event-factory/event-factory.service.ts`

Validates a payload against the domain's Zod schema, wraps it in an `EventEnvelope`, and publishes it to the `EventBus`. All domain mutation files call `eventFactory.publish()` through this service. Gate enforcement throughout the codebase assumes this file is intact.

### `event-bus.service.ts`

**Path:** `apps/api/src/lib/event-bus.service.ts`

In-process pub/sub service. Exposes two methods: `subscribe(eventName, handler)` and `publishEnvelope(envelope)`. Listener files wire domain events to broadcast helpers through this service.

### `schemas/_base.ts`

**Path:** `apps/api/src/lib/event-factory/schemas/_base.ts`

Defines the `EventEnvelope` and `AnyPayload` Zod schemas that every domain schema slice extends. The barrel `schemas/_index.ts` exports these alongside domain slices.

### `entity-cache.ts`

**Path:** `apps/web/src/lib/cache/entity-cache.ts`

Provides `applyEntityCreate`, `applyEntityUpdate`, and `applyEntityDelete` — the only legal cache writers for entity arrays on the frontend. Gate #4 enforces that no file other than projection files calls `queryClient.setQueryData` for entity arrays directly.

### `state-cache.ts`

**Path:** `apps/web/src/lib/cache/state-cache.ts`

The single frontend dispatch point for real-time events. `applyRealtimeEventToCache(eventName, payload, queryClient)` looks up the event name in `domainDispatchers` and calls the matching handler. The `WebSocketProvider` routes every incoming socket event through this function.

### `WebSocketProvider.tsx`

**Path:** `apps/web/src/providers/WebSocketProvider.tsx`

Connects to socket.io on mount, then loops over `getAllWsBindings()` to register a `routeEvent()` handler for each domain event. Gate #8 enforces that this file never imports `entity-cache` or calls `applyEntity*()` directly — all cache writes must flow through `state-cache`.

### `entity-projections/_types.ts`

**Path:** `packages/shared/src/entity-projections/_types.ts`

Exports the `EntityProjectionEntry`, `ProjectionFn`, and `QueryKey` TypeScript types used by every domain's `entity-projections/<domain>.ts` file.

### Vite Proxy Shims

Three thin `export * from "./_index"` files that bridge Vite's directory-import resolution. They must exist for Vite to resolve barrel imports correctly; their content is never changed.

| Shim               | Path                                                 |
| ------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------- |
| Query keys         | `apps/web/src/lib/query-keys/index.ts`               |
| Domain dispatchers | `apps/web/src/lib/cache/domain-dispatchers/index.ts` |
| WS bindings        | `apps/web/src/providers/ws-bindings/index.ts`        |

### `AGENTS.md` and `CLAUDE.md`

**Paths:** project root (`AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`)

`AGENTS.md` teaches AI coding agents the One River rules — the constraints every contributor (human or AI) must follow. `CLAUDE.md` auto-loads `AGENTS.md` into the Claude Code context on every session start. Neither file should be modified; overwriting them removes the gate-awareness contract for AI agents working in the repo.

---

## Domain Files

Domain files are written by `rivergen gen` once per domain. Subsequent `gen` runs skip files that already exist unless `--force` is passed. The examples below use `task` as the domain key; substitute your domain name throughout.

Files in this section fall into three groups by the amount of developer work required after generation:

- **Zero changes needed:** `listener.ts`, `domain-dispatchers/<domain>.ts`, `ws-bindings/<domain>.ts`
- **Minimal changes (field-level):** `router.ts`, `broadcast.ts`, `query-keys/<domain>.ts`
- **Substantial filling required:** `mutations.ts`, `schemas/<domain>.ts`, `<domain>-projections.ts`, `use-<domain>.ts`, `entity-projections/<domain>.ts`, `<domain>.witness.ts`

---

### `router.ts`

**Path:** `apps/api/src/task/task.router.ts`

Express router with HTTP endpoints. RiverGen writes route declarations that call the mutation functions imported from `task.mutations.ts`. Developers add auth middleware where required; the business logic itself lives entirely in `mutations.ts`.

---

### `mutations.ts`

**Path:** `apps/api/src/task/task.mutations.ts`

Business logic layer. RiverGen writes function stubs with `// TODO` comments marking the DB call site and the payload field list, plus a `eventFactory.publish()` stub with the correct event name and envelope structure. Developers fill:

- The actual DB call (Prisma or other ORM)
- Input validation
- The complete payload object passed to `eventFactory.publish()`

Gate #1 scans this file to verify that `eventFactory.publish()` is called for every mutation and that the call structure is intact.

---

### `broadcast.ts`

**Path:** `apps/api/src/task/task.broadcast.ts`

socket.io emit helper. RiverGen extracts room resolution logic from the domain's room template variables and writes a single routing function that resolves the room from the payload and calls `io.to(room).emit()`.

For domains with `visibilityField` **and** `privateRoomTemplate` set, the generator produces a complete `isPrivate` guard with the correct private room expression — no developer editing required. If `visibilityField` is set but `privateRoomTemplate` is omitted, the PRIVATE branch contains a `TODO_private_room` placeholder that must be replaced manually (or by adding `privateRoomTemplate` to the spec and re-running `gen --force`).

For simple room configurations (no `visibilityField`) the file requires no changes after generation.

Gate #5 scans this file to verify room scoping is present and no event is broadcast to a catch-all room.

---

### `listener.ts`

**Path:** `apps/api/src/lib/event-bus-listeners/task.listener.ts`

Wires `EventBus` subscriptions to the broadcast helper. RiverGen writes the complete file — it is pure structural wiring with no business logic. This file typically requires no changes after generation.

Gate #2 scans this file to verify all domain events have a matching `eventBus.subscribe()` call.

---

### `schemas/task.ts`

**Path:** `apps/api/src/lib/event-factory/schemas/task.ts`

Zod schema entries for each event payload, registered as `.strict()` objects. RiverGen writes a stub containing only the `entityId` field plus a `// TODO` comment. Developers fill all actual payload fields.

Gate #7 enforces that every schema entry uses `.strict()`. Gate #12 (Layer 1) validates that every field listed in the witness's `requiredFields` is present in this schema.

---

### `task-projections.ts`

**Path:** `apps/web/src/lib/projections/task-projections.ts`

Frontend projection functions. For CRUD events RiverGen writes `applyTaskCreated`, `applyTaskUpdated`, and `applyTaskDeleted`, each calling the corresponding `applyEntityCreate`, `applyEntityUpdate`, or `applyEntityDelete` from `entity-cache`. For signal events (events not ending in `.created`, `.updated`, or `.deleted` — e.g. `task.assigned`, `task.status-changed`) RiverGen also writes `applyTaskAssigned`, `applyTaskStatusChanged`, and so on — one stub per signal event.

All stubs contain a `// TODO` context placeholder. Developers fill the context object with the correct room-scope fields (e.g. `{ projectId: payload.projectId as string }`) so the projection writes to the same cache key the hook is watching.

Gate #4 scans this file to verify that cache writes go through the `applyEntity*` helpers and not direct `queryClient.setQueryData` calls.

---

### `use-task.ts`

**Path:** `apps/web/src/hooks/use-task.ts`

React Query hooks: `useTaskList`, `useCreateTask`, `useUpdateTask`, `useDeleteTask`. RiverGen writes complete stubs including:

- `onMutate` with ghost insertion and `clientTempId` stamping
- `onError` with rollback
- `onSuccess` intentionally omitted (the system relies on real-time events for cache reconciliation, not HTTP response data)

**Hook signatures include room scope parameters** derived from the spec's `room.template`. For `"project:${projectId}"` the mutation and list hooks all require `projectId: string` as their first argument:

```typescript
useTaskList(projectId: string, options?)
useCreateTask(projectId: string)
useUpdateTask(projectId: string)
useDeleteTask(projectId: string)
```

The room params are forwarded to `taskKeys.list({ projectId })` inside `onMutate` so the optimistic update writes to the correct scoped cache entry. Call sites pass the room variable from their component's route context.

Developers fill the API call URLs. The `onMutate` list key shape is generated correctly and rarely needs editing — if the room template uses a different variable (e.g. `workspaceId`) the generated key already reflects it.

Gate #9 scans this file for the presence of `onMutate` on every mutation hook. Gate #10 scans for the absence of `onSuccess` (the ban on `onSuccess` for entity mutations is a hard rule).

---

### `domain-dispatchers/task.ts`

**Path:** `apps/web/src/lib/cache/domain-dispatchers/task.ts`

A map of event name strings to projection functions, for example `{ "task.created": applyTaskCreated, "task.updated": applyTaskUpdated, "task.deleted": applyTaskDeleted }`. RiverGen writes the complete file. This file requires no developer changes.

Gate #3 scans this file to verify that every event in the domain has a dispatcher entry.

---

### `ws-bindings/task.ts`

**Path:** `apps/web/src/providers/ws-bindings/task.ts`

Exports `getTaskWsBindings()` returning the array of event name strings for this domain. RiverGen writes the complete file. This file requires no developer changes.

---

### `entity-projections/task.ts`

**Path:** `packages/shared/src/entity-projections/task.ts`

`EntityProjectionEntry` map for cache key routing — tells `applyEntityCreate/Update` which query key factory to use when locating the entity list in the cache. RiverGen generates a room-var-aware key factory automatically from the spec's `room.template`: for `"project:${projectId}"` the generated `onCreate.required` entry reads the `projectId` from the projection context, producing `["tasks", "list", projectId]`.

This key shape is designed to match the hook's `onMutate` list key exactly. In most cases no developer edits are needed. If the entity's query key structure differs from the generated default (e.g. a nested key with additional segments), update the `required` factory to match.

---

### `query-keys/task.ts`

**Path:** `apps/web/src/lib/query-keys/task.ts`

Query key factories: `taskKeys.all()`, `taskKeys.list(ctx)`, `taskKeys.detail(id)`. RiverGen generates these from the domain's room template variables. In most cases the generated output is correct and requires no developer changes.

---

### `task.witness.ts`

**Path:** `apps/web/src/witness/task.witness.ts`

The field continuity contract for the task domain (`taskWitness`). RiverGen writes a typed scaffold with `// TODO` stubs for the `lifecycle()` body. Developers fill:

- The `TaskPayload` TypeScript interface
- `requiredFields` — the fields that must be present in the Zod schema
- `testPayloads` — representative payloads for gate validation
- `lifecycle()` assertions — the runtime checks that verify the payload shape in tests
- `signals{}` — any domain-specific signal fields

Gate #12 validates this file and cross-checks `requiredFields` against `schemas/task.ts`.

---

## Barrel Files

Barrel files are regenerated on every `rivergen gen` run and always overwritten. They never contain user code. It is safe to re-run `gen` at any time — no barrel file will lose developer changes because developers are not expected to modify them.

### `schemas/_index.ts`

**Path:** `apps/api/src/lib/event-factory/schemas/_index.ts`

Exports all domain schema slices alongside the base schemas. The `EventFactory` service imports from this barrel.

### `domain-dispatchers/_index.ts`

**Path:** `apps/web/src/lib/cache/domain-dispatchers/_index.ts`

Merges all per-domain dispatcher maps into a single `domainDispatchers` object. `state-cache.ts` imports `domainDispatchers` from this barrel.

### `ws-bindings/_index.ts`

**Path:** `apps/web/src/providers/ws-bindings/_index.ts`

Exports `getAllWsBindings()`, which concatenates the results of every domain's `get<Domain>WsBindings()` call. `WebSocketProvider.tsx` imports `getAllWsBindings` from this barrel.

### `query-keys/_index.ts`

**Path:** `apps/web/src/lib/query-keys/_index.ts`

Re-exports all domain query key factories from a single import point.

### `entity-projections/_index.ts`

**Path:** `packages/shared/src/entity-projections/_index.ts`

Exports the combined `ENTITY_PROJECTIONS` map, merging all per-domain `EntityProjectionEntry` maps. The projection system uses this map to route real-time events to the correct cache key.
